title: DLRS .life Asset Lifecycle
description: Lifecycle metadata for `.life` packages and the assets they ship (v0.8 asset-architecture epic, sub-issue #102). Defines four document shapes via `$defs`: `package_lifecycle` (top-level fields in `life-package.json`), `asset_lifecycle` (block embedded in each `genesis/<asset_id>.genesis.json`), `mutation_event` (one record of an append-only `lifecycle/<asset_id>.mutations.jsonl` log), and `cascade_index` (the package-wide `lifecycle/cascade_index.json` file mapping source-input refs to derived-asset ids). See docs/LIFE_LIFECYCLE_SPEC.md for the normative text. The schema itself does NOT directly validate any single file at the document root; it only exports re-usable shapes referenced from other schemas and from the lifecycle test suite.

lifecycle_state.description: Package-level lifecycle states. `active` = current and serviceable. `superseded` = newer version exists; loaders SHOULD prefer the successor but MAY still load this one for replay/audit. `expired` = `expires_at` reached; loaders MUST refuse interactive use. `withdrawn` = subject revoked authorisation; loaders MUST refuse all use. `frozen` = read-only loadable (no new writes; assets stay reachable). `memorial` = subject deceased and the package was flipped per decision D4 — read-only loadable by default, may be extended by an authorised executor.

asset_lifecycle_state.description: Asset-level lifecycle states. Superset of `lifecycle_state` plus `tainted` (decision D3=B): when a source input is withdrawn, derived assets are NOT deleted (which would break the audit chain) but flipped to `tainted`. Compliant runtimes MUST refuse to use `tainted` assets. Note: `expired` and `memorial` apply only at package level (an individual asset cannot independently be in those states); they are excluded here.

supersedes_entry.description: A single predecessor reference. Decision D2=C: forking (one predecessor → many descendants) is allowed; merging (many predecessors → one descendant) is forbidden, so each `supersedes_entry` array MUST have at most one element when the lifecycle event is `package_versioned`. `package_id` and `version` are human/operator readable; `sha256` is the machine-trusted identity (decision D1=D dual-identity).

sha256.description: Content hash of the predecessor's primary metadata (life-package.json bytes).

memorial_metadata.description: Populated when `lifecycle_state == "memorial"`. Decision D4=C+(a)+(c): trigger MAY come from any of `executor`, `next_of_kin`, or `court_order`. The 7-day reverse-attestation dispute period (Appendix B of LIFE_ASSET_ARCHITECTURE.md) applies between `triggered_at` and `triggered_at + P7D`; loaders MUST NOT treat the package as memorial-confirmed until the dispute window has elapsed (or until the subject reverse-attests, in which case the trigger is cancelled).

trigger_actor.description: Identifier of the human/legal person who triggered the memorial state (e.g. executor's email/DID, next-of-kin's identifier, court reference number).

evidence_ref.description: Optional path inside `consent/` or `audit/` pointing to evidence (death certificate, court order). RECOMMENDED for `court_order`.

dispute_window_ends_at.description: `triggered_at + 7 days` (UTC). After this instant the memorial state is confirmed (no further reverse-attestation possible).

dispute_window_status.description: `open` during the 7-day window. `expired` once it has passed without reverse-attestation. `cancelled_by_subject` if the subject reverse-attested via the v0.7 `withdrawal_endpoint` to cancel a malicious trigger; the package's `lifecycle_state` MUST then be reset to `active` (not memorial).

executor_can_extend.description: True iff an authorised executor MAY sign new versions after the dispute window expires (e.g. memorial events, archival publishing). Decision D4 sub-clause (c).

package_lifecycle.description: Top-level package-lifecycle fields. These are added to `life-package.json` (next to v0.7's existing fields). The companion update to `schemas/life-package.schema.json` is part of the v0.8 schema-integration PR (out of scope for this spec PR); validators MAY apply this fragment standalone in the meantime.

version.description: Semantic version of THIS package. Decision D1=D human-side. Combined with the package's content sha256 (recorded in the manifest), the (semver, sha256) pair is the dual identity used by lifecycle.

supersedes.description: Predecessor packages this version replaces. Decision D2=C: forking allowed, merging forbidden. Therefore this array MUST have at most ONE element for any non-merge version event. The schema enforces `maxItems: 1` to make merge attempts statically rejectable.

frozen.description: True iff the package is read-only loadable. MUST be true when `lifecycle_state` is `frozen` or `memorial`.

memorial_metadata.description: Populated only when `lifecycle_state == "memorial"`; null otherwise. Conditional rules below enforce this.

recommended_re_consent_after.description: Decision D5=C, soft re-consent reminder. NOT a hard expiry (the v0.7 `expires_at` field still owns hard expiry). Loaders SHOULD surface a non-blocking banner once this instant is reached.

asset_lifecycle.description: The lifecycle block embedded inside each `genesis/<asset_id>.genesis.json` next to the v0.8 Genesis fields. Captures per-asset version, predecessors, mutation log pointer, hard expiry, and current state including the asset-only `tainted` value.

version.description: Semantic version of THIS asset (independent of the enclosing package's version).

supersedes.description: Predecessor asset_ids this version replaces. Decision D2=C: at most one predecessor. Items use the same asset_id pattern enforced by `mutation_event.asset_id`, `mutation_event.successor_asset_id`, `cascade_index.derived_assets`, and `genesis.schema.json::asset_id` for cross-schema consistency.

mutation_log_ref.description: Path inside the `.life` zip (e.g. `lifecycle/voice-master-v1.mutations.jsonl`). Required when the asset has any non-creation mutation events. The referenced file MUST be JSONL where each line validates against `#/$defs/mutation_event`. The pattern explicitly rejects `..` segments to prevent path traversal out of the `lifecycle/` directory (matching the convention enforced by `life-package.schema.json` `contents[].path`).

expires_at.description: Hard expiry for this asset specifically (independent of the package-level `expires_at`). Loaders MUST refuse to use this asset after this instant.

tainted_reason.description: REQUIRED when `state == "tainted"`. Free-form short explanation (e.g. `source_input recording-2025-01-12.wav was withdrawn at 2026-04-01T08:00:00Z`).

mutation_event.description: One record in `lifecycle/<asset_id>.mutations.jsonl`. Append-only: no record may be removed or rewritten. The audit log (`audit/events.jsonl`) is the canonical event source; mutation logs are a denormalised per-asset view to make withdrawal cascades and reproducibility audits cheap.

action.description: Mutation kinds. `asset_created` = first creation event. `input_added` / `input_withdrawn` = source-input set changed. `asset_retrained` = method or config bumped, new sha256 produced. `state_changed` = `state` transitioned (active→tainted, etc.). `superseded_by` = a successor version exists.

cascade_index.description: The `lifecycle/cascade_index.json` file: a package-wide map from each source-input ref (recording, text, dataset, base_model, etc.) to every asset that consumed it. Used by withdrawal-cascade implementations to find all derived assets affected by a single source withdrawal in O(1) instead of O(assets * inputs).

entries.description: One entry per distinct source input ref. The `derived_assets` list is the closure: assets directly OR transitively derived from the input.
